summary:
Only include two concise sentences focusing strictly on clinically significant facts related directly to treating thyroid dysfunction in children.

The treatment of choice for childhood thyrotoxicosis is antithyroid medication, particularly carbimazole or methimazole, which offer an effective and safe initial approach aligned with pediatric guidelines. Radioactive iodine is avoided in young children due to risks like induced Graves’ orbitopathy, while surgical options are limited to exceptional cases when medications fail. 

Note: This summary does not mention specific dosing regimens unless they were part of core decision-making logic — here, only general therapeutic principles matter per instructions.
